| | | Friend,

Can you help Team Corey get on the ballot to defeat radical leftist Tim Kaine?

Corey Stewart is the only candidate who will defeat Kaine and fight for Trump's #AmericaFirst Agenda in the U.S. Senate.

It's _really_ simple! Just print out the petition form & instructions below. Petition Form - [http://bit.ly/2DQZi0c](https://coreystewart.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4bda7ecfd918a7c6eeeede5d2&id=b792b8ddc1&e=b8a7e4c8d6) Instructions - [http://bit.ly/2CdmANG](https://coreystewart.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4bda7ecfd918a7c6eeeede5d2&id=01977eff23&e=b8a7e4c8d6)

Make sure you read and follow the instructions closely!

Need help? Need forms delivered? We can have one of our regional team members come assist you. [Sign up here](https://coreystewart.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4bda7ecfd918a7c6eeeede5d2&id=99803d93aa&e=b8a7e4c8d6) and they'll contact you ASAP!

Want to join fellow supporters this Saturday at locations around the state collecting signatures. [Stewart for Senate Signature Saturday!](https://coreystewart.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4bda7ecfd918a7c6eeeede5d2&id=ed5e640504&e=b8a7e4c8d6)
January 6th, 2018
10 AM - 5 PM
RSVP by clicking [here](https://coreystewart.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4bda7ecfd918a7c6eeeede5d2&id=1435e89955&e=b8a7e4c8d6) Getting on the ballot in Virginia is no easy task. Each campaign must collect thousands of signatures in every part of the state.

[Join #TeamCorey staff and volunteers at one of the many locations in Virginia](https://coreystewart.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4bda7ecfd918a7c6eeeede5d2&id=05b4b6e947&e=b8a7e4c8d6).

name: The White House
subject: ‘You gave us a deadline’
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5aa62c8e7251d85732b8f0916debcf4d5f8645649ed7696ab3481e0f4f99f00dec13de5dfaf31d6d86caf0994b84a779481c2c37ab908257

‘You gave us a deadline’

“We’re all working in an effort to develop an immigration reform plan that will serve the interests of the American workers,” President Donald J. Trump said yesterday.

The President’s remarks came after he and Vice President Mike Pence sat down with a Republican Senate working group on immigration in the Roosevelt Room of the White House.

Several quotes after the meeting reveal what 2018 could hold for one of the President’s signature issues:

- Vice President Pence: “We’re going to enforce the laws of this country for the citizens of this country. . . . We’re going to do it with a big heart.”
- Sen. James Lankford (R-OK) to the President: “Immigration issues are very hard, they’re very emotional. But there’s been no deadline . . . You gave us a deadline.”
- President Trump: “If we have support from the Democrats, I think DACA is going to be terrific. . . We need protection. We need the wall. We need all of those things.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5aa62c8e7251d857efc9c34e5003808778f4e623eba6ca37a397f6d2931716962afaabe20c89f19cd886b44ff16e6fd8d374a1e44336ea21
Read how the President thinks bipartisan immigration reform can happen in 2018.

Vice President Pence: ‘Trump will not be silent’

In an op-ed for The Washington Post, Vice President Pence drew a sharp distinction between how President Trump and former President Barack Obama handled mass Iranian protests against that country’s oppressive regime.

“Where his predecessor stayed silent in 2009,” the Vice President wrote, “Trump swiftly offered the Iranian people America’s unwavering support. He has also committed to provide assistance in the days ahead.”

The Vice President’s closing lines remind us that America is strongest on the world stage when it stands behind its values. “That is the essence of American leadership.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5aa62c8e7251d8573bf982933cfd7a92aedefb8147d1c80330ba0d7e116979a11582913c6cfeb8c0ddee46894fff223bf0ba381b2b3928fb
Read the Vice President’s op-ed in full.

Dow breaks 25,000 for the first time ever

CNBC and others reported yesterday that the Dow Jones Industrial Average has cleared the 25,000 mark for the first time—“tying the fastest 1,000-point move in its history,” writes Fred Imbert.

A quotable snippet from Imbert’s piece: “While it’s symbolically important, the real story is never just a number. It’s the underlying strength that is pushing markets this high,” said Steve Claussen, vice president of trader strategy at ETRADE.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5aa62c8e7251d85713a508e89d729aec98998d1ade243f0e1623e27b4c6819b88c4ada68b83aff0f4ebffd083fe15ec4acc3168952f1f01a
Learn more about why the markets are soaring.

Photo of the Day

Vice President Mike Pence and Mrs. Karen Pence host faith leaders for dinner at the Naval Observatory | January 4, 2018 (Official White House Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS TODAY

This afternoon, President Trump will depart the White House en route to Camp David, where he will host bi-cameral Congressional Leadership for a small-group discussion of 2018 legislative priorities and the successful passage of tax cuts and reform.

COMING UP

On Monday, President Trump will travel to Nashville, Tennessee, to address the American Farm Bureau Federation’s Annual Meeting—the first time since 1992 an American president has spoken at the event.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5aa62c8e7251d85783d0c9b1c7f27e599bc2aa69f3dffd61528ef0ba463892f67011139640110fe122ca0170d3c4079ab81351d48a3df87a
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5aa62c8e7251d857c3b5818b20e5d304c1172cb594ca7b7d21fee1cc41676b856dbddbbcd7530414a1490ca248316deec72767070592342a
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4272663fcb59f85fc070e8e115acb2a0f01f6ce7aba50c00787df092239f327efa73431edfe13831a1c2db2a2a0694cf5
Unsubscribe

We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c69a6/24c69fd9/13d29832f/18932d27/1214384842/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEM06N_digital_email_20180105_TrainingProgram_inact&source=WQEFEM06N_digital_email_20180105_TrainingProgram_inact")

Peter,

What do you do when you want to run for office but your only campaign experience is watching _The West Wing_?

That's the dilemma facing hundreds of pro-choice Democratic women who are putting their name on the ballot for the first time this year.

This is why EMILY's List exists -- we're here to help make sure these courageous women have the tools they need to run and win. This year, we're holding more specialized trainings for candidates than ever before and rolling out regular online trainings.

How many women we can reach depends on your support.

**[Donate $3 to help first-time candidates get the tools they need to run and win.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c69a6/24c69fd9/13d29832f/18932d20/1214384842/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180103_email_TrainingProgram?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+firsttime+candidates+ge&utm_campaign=WQEFEM06N_digital_email_20180105_TrainingProgram_inact&source=WQEFEM06N_digital_email_20180105_TrainingProgram_inact")**

We've already seen the impact our candidate trainings can have on the outcome of elections.

My team went to Virginia in July and trained several women running for House of Delegates seats -- and in November, 13 of the women we endorsed won their races, with 11 of them flipping seats from red to blue. AND we sent the first openly transgender woman, first Latinas, and first Asian American women to the Virginia House of Delegates.

This year, we plan to criss-cross the country and hold even more advanced trainings for pro-choice Democratic women candidates.

Let's change the face of politics across the country like we did in Virginia.

**Donate $3 to help train women running for office for the first time and send them the support they need to win:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Train-More-Women](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c69a6/24c69fd9/13d29832f/18932d21/1214384842/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180103_email_TrainingProgram?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgTrainMoreWomen&utm_campaign=WQEFEM06N_digital_email_20180105_TrainingProgram_inact&source=WQEFEM06N_digital_email_20180105_TrainingProgram_inact")**

Thanks,

Mũthoni Wambu Kraal
Vice President of National Outreach and Training, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c69a6/24c69fd9/13d29832f/18932d22/1214384842/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180103_email_TrainingProgram?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFEM06N_digital_email_20180105_TrainingProgram_inact&source=WQEFEM06N_digital_email_20180105_TrainingProgram_inact")

My team went to Virginia in July and trained several women running for House of Delegates seats -- and in November, 13 of the women we endorsed won their races, with 11 of them flipping seats from red to blue. AND we sent the first openly transgender woman, first Latinas, and first Asian American women to the Virginia House of Delegates. This year, we plan to criss-cross the country and hold even more advanced trainings for pro-choice Democratic women candidates. Let's change the face of politics across the country like we did in Virginia. name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: The end of the sham Trump-Pence election commission
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Big news, Friend: The sham Trump-Pence election commission
shut down on Wednesday. People around the country recognized the
commission's illegitimate mission -- a way to disenfranchise voters --
and they came together to stymie the commission at every turn.

That’s in large part due to the actions of Legit members who
recognized an opportunity for victory and never gave up.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/4546?t=1002&akid=528%2E356062%2E8QwO7v ]This is a victory for legitimacy. But we can’t let up now -- not
with the 2018 elections around the corner and more victories
attainable. Contribute $3 so we can
keep pushing hard for legitimate elections.

The sham voter commission was nothing more than a way to lay the
groundwork for nationalized voter suppression -- another attempt in a
decades-long GOP effort to take away the rights of voters in this
country to cast meaningful ballots.

With pressure from LegitAction members, we helped shut the commission
down.

What this group is doing is working, Friend. LegitAction was
founded to meaningfully improve election legitimacy, even with
President Trump in the White House. This marks a major victory over
something that seemed inevitable only a few months ago. Now, we have a
responsibility to keep it up.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/4546?t=1003&akid=528%2E356062%2E8QwO7v ]Make sure that we can sustain this pressure throughout 2018. Make a
contribution of $3 right
now.

Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today. It's making
a difference.

Russ Feingold
Founder, LegitAction

subject: Nearly 500 Hoosiers
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
|
---
| **_Nearly 500 Hoosiers_** It's officially 2018. As the calendar turned to 2018 on New Year's, my mind was racing. Hoosier Republicans have a packed year, with lots of work ahead of us:
* In May, we'll go to the polls to select our Republican nominees in races up and down the ballot.
* In June, we'll come together in Evansville for our Indiana Republican State Convention, where we'll nominate our 2018 statewide team and get geared up for a busy election season.
* In November we'll go to the polls again -- and this time, we'll be sending Joe Donnelly official notice that his ineffective services in the U.S. Senate are no longer needed.

Between all of those dates, we'll have a packed schedule of events across the state that help us work toward victory in November. And the first big event to get us there is [Congress of Counties](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=076e6e52ad&e=1acd9bb3d6), our two-day political training event for local Republican party leaders, grassroots volunteers, candidates and future candidates.

I'm excited to share that we now have NEARLY 500 Hoosiers registered to attend Congress of Counties. Registration to the full event is now officially sold out, with an incredible response from Hoosiers throughout the entire state.

While registration to attend the full Congress of Counties event is sold out, we do still have a few remaining seats for [our special evening reception](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0ffbc3aefd&e=1acd9bb3d6) on Friday, Jan. 12. The reception is honoring and sponsored by******Secretary of State Connie Lawson, Treasurer of State Kelly Mitchell** and**Auditor of State Tera Klutz** , with special guests **Governor Eric Holcomb** and **Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch**. It's set to be a great event (with[ live music from Indy Nile](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b769dcb2f7&e=1acd9bb3d6)), and we'd love for you to join us (but [register now](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d3f247d688&e=1acd9bb3d6) before we sell out for that, too)!

Whether it's at a Lincoln Day dinner, a local party meeting or Congress of Counties next weekend, I look forward to seeing you in 2018, as we work together across the state toward victory!

-Kyle Hupfer

Then on Wednesday, **Governor Holcomb** helped kickoff the 2018 legislative session with the 14th Annual Statehouse Prayer Service, which brings legislators and other Hoosiers together to reflect on the big challenges and issues that will face them throughout the legislative session.

_**The Hill: "Medicaid is GOP target in 2018"**_
[**Add your name: demand Republicans not slash safety net programs >>**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d723/2b07114c/a44b4d42/731a157f/2048106692/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/tell-the-gop-no-cuts?source=PT-20180105-Alert_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")
Team,

_The Hill_ just reported that Medicaid is "squarely in the bull's-eye" of the GOP's agenda this month -- and that Medicare could be on the chopping block too.

The GOP's tax scam adds so much to the deficit -- more than $1.5 trillion -- but now Republicans are _shamelessly_ trying to scapegoat these essential safety net programs to pay for their tax scam.

It's unthinkable that politicians would try to pay for tax breaks for billionaires and big corporations with cuts that target seniors and low-income families -- but that's where the Republican party is right now.

[**We need your help to fight back -- add your name right now to demand Republicans keep their hands off of Medicaid and Medicare.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d723/2b07114c/a44b4d42/731a157f/2048106692/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/tell-the-gop-no-cuts?source=PT-20180105-Alert_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Republicans are worried about their prospects for November. And that means they'll push bad policy after bad policy to keep their base -- and their wealthy donors -- happy.

That's why it's so crucial that we get out in front of this and show the GOP the wall of resistance they will face if they come after our vital safety net programs.

**Add your name right now to show Republicans you are ready to fight the GOP 's attempts to cut Medicaid and Medicare:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/tell-the-gop-no-cuts**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d723/2b07114c/a44b4d42/731a157f/2048106692/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/tell-the-gop-no-cuts?source=PT-20180105-Alert_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Thank you,

Team DSCC

**A quick note on donations, team:**

When we say that we're counting on grassroots donors to protect Senate Democrats, we're not kidding. In fact, a full 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We don't have the Koch brothers in our corner -- we have you. And every gift you give the DSCC is a gift we use to propel our Senate candidates to victory.

It's unthinkable that politicians would try to pay for tax breaks for billionaires and big corporations with cuts that target seniors and low-income families -- but that's where the Republican party is right now. "=" Republicans are worried about their prospects for November. And that means they'll push bad policy after bad policy to keep their base -- and their wealthy donors -- happy. That's why it's so crucial that we get out in front of this and show the GOP the wall of resistance they will face if they come after our vital safety net programs. subject: Forrester: Hassan Did Nothing to Stop Opioid Crisis
body:
**Forrester: Hassan Did Nothing to Stop Opioid Crisis**
**For Immediate Release:** 1/5/2018
**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, (202) 321-5310
CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement following Senator Maggie Hassan (D-NH) politicizing the opioid crisis in the Democrat's Weekly Address:
“It takes an especially cynical politician to politicize the nation’s opioid crisis. But Senator Maggie Hassan has plumbed the depths of decent civil discourse. She used her one-minute of national notoriety to drive a political wedge in an issue that knows no partisanship and doesn’t discriminate among its victims. Once again, Maggie Hassan has brought ill-repute to the Granite State. During her tenure as Governor of New Hampshire, the state’s opioid crisis spiraled out-of-control and she did precious little to stop it. It bears noting that now that she has left Concord, we have finally begun to stem the tide of overdose deaths for the first time in half a decade, when she was first elected.”
**BACKGROUND**
Hassan calls for more spending on opioid crisis in Democratic address[http://www.wmur.com/article/hassan-calls-for-more-spending-on-opioid-crisis-in-democratic-address/14762761](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbR-2BncdYrjYGa-2BcdGvpvk-2BU22PZYsdje4e-2Fq6AmawU8XR2sPVX4ZqNMDkT9cUA2v8ADmkS3qvTExFqB0kbiAha5j1da8ch2tuKcBMEzI8ZtUNY3pHS-2FIl1CG8suuUfwcX3z1-2FVyniXImEsRmkKG2tIEE-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M-2BD40OvzHvyFW1E7zLjAXWRiUAYeHR2Jua8Kk9T2iO7epM3-2BAU6X-2F6GprF8sVqqRMHJMlV-2FHSh4qB1qKE8bV06q7SUOQatYOWO8szwFofKR8YIQAztfpj7s8CdXMjkmTnMwqiakSR60-2FFi3EPduYttMfafNZPQUTgvIOHXi2JSiuD9vq39ZyRvFqe81OTjvu-2Fp96byuLwdplOX-2BXHwLcOJuzshPj48kaMm44jlNsYUgg8DVft-2BbTQLU82ddTdXqhalOeJt17uHFoKtLgghf27PQ-3D-3D) Editorial Boards: Hassan Playing Politics With Heroin Response[https://americarisingpac.org/editorial-boards-hassan-playing-politics-with-heroin-response/](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyAE0wjhkDkiqE0u3BE6JlQVJqocEIXTgkFdosUhFDK1ZDpTVKhSBDZK0WZtpnPAl45Bbho6Ojv-2BmXR1rp80rbFyZGvRIyi5TepOFxqKldz6Oo-2F8C8PP62iFaO7GxncmTEg-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M-2BD40OvzHvyFW1E7zLjAXWRiUAYeHR2Jua8Kk9T2iO7epM3-2BAU6X-2F6GprF8sVqqRMHJMlV-2FHSh4qB1qKE8bV06p2JrtaguV0aKMFA8h5DRChj-2Bc6rAyY06imnvmSIPwFVYKmoUXphHqwa1-2BqBHua4uZfNBHL2iRNBEmLvc03IPqGGGwsJYpVmpIJiu2C5Jd63q4MgiMA1uzxPs0VR3Nho8sH7zNBq1bSt9pn6uiZNWdRFWGYYkGcWrVZDB5Zm9rvPt4Iml8wBJzkIkeaUzHHvLg-3D-3D)

| | Derek --

Thank you again for being a part of Onward Together in 2017. With your help, we were able to support eleven groups doing critical work toward making our country stronger and more fair.

You've already been introduced to the first round of organizations. Today, I wanted to tell you a little bit about some of the newer members of the Onward Together family:

The organizers at **iVote** worked with partners on the ground to get automatic voter registration on the ballot in Nevada in 2017. 450 brand-new civic leaders and activists attended **The Arena 's** very first summit -- and they're on track to train 5,000 people by the end of 2018. At the **Alliance for Youth Action** , young people are using local organizing to address economic inequality, attacks on voting rights, and more. The team at **Latino Victory Fund** is working to elect more Latino candidates at every level of government. More than 400 students and leaders met last month at the **Voto Latino** Power Summit to share resources and tools for national advocacy. And with the help of **Collective Future** , 23 African-American candidates have been elected to local, state, and federal offices since August 2016.

Along with Indivisible, Color of Change, Emerge America, Swing Left, and Run for Something, I know these groups will continue to do the incredible work of making our democracy stronger in 2018, and I'm proud to be on their team. We're only able to support these organizations because of you, so one more time, thank you. **If you're still with us in 2018,[add your name right now, and let's do even more together.](https://secure.onwardtogether.org/page/m/c0879a1/45580a18/4b52f4b5/57a5169e/4142532778/VEsE/ "https://secure.onwardtogether.org/page/s/onward?utm_medium=email&utm_source=onward&utm_content=2+-+add+your+name+right+now+and+lets+do+even&utm_campaign=20180105_fr_ty&source=20180105_fr_ty")**

**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

**2017 was a great year for NH Republicans. Under Republican leadership, we’ve made some great strides forward. Under Republican leadership, we’ve made some great strides forward. In case you missed it, 2018 promises to be even better as we grow our majorities in the House and Senate, send Governor Sununu back to the corner office, and fill those Congressional seats. ** Here at the NHGOP, we are getting ready for our annual meeting on Saturday, January 27th at Bow High School at 32 White Rock Hill Road in Bow. Credentialing for voting will be from 7:30 a.m. to 10 a.m. and the meeting will start promptly at 9 a.m. Meeting packets will be going out next week, but just to give you some highlights of the day, we will have guest speakers, consider changes to our by-laws, and present awards that recognize the hard work and dedication of Republicans. While you wait for your packet, you may want to check out a, which can be found on our website at . Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle!
